title: lotononis cytisoides var. brevifolia
intro: Lotononis cytisoides var. brevifolia, also known as the "bushman's poison", is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to southern Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used in the form of a poultice or salve to treat various ailments.
